name: Finite Integrity Theorem
conclusion: operation is C iff Delta=0; otherwise commutator-visible V

name: Proof-Logged Public Solver Corollary
assumption: solver trace consists only of public resolution/CDCL proof steps
conclusion: trace is C and cannot extract e33

name: Parity-Solver Classification
conclusion: solver enters X by explicit extractor-adjoin

name: Mixed-System Integrity Audit
assumption: solver uses extensions, cutting planes, SDP, or spectral steps
conclusion: each step must be certified C/V or marked X
status: contract built; proof depends on supplied trace

name: Algorithmic Universality Theorem
assumption: every polynomial solver has a faithful polynomial integrity trace
conclusion: any solver deciding e33-obstructed instances must contain V or X

name: Conditional P-vs-NP No-Escape
assumption: Algorithmic Universality + X excluded from public-P traces + SAT-complete hidden e33 family
conclusion: public-integral polynomial solving fails; full separation follows for the encoded family if SAT-complete
